title: quercus nigra var. triloba
intro: Quercus nigra var. triloba, also known as the Black Oak, is a deciduous tree native to North America. The medicinal properties of Black Oak include its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins, and its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Black Oak are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Black Oak, such as teas and infusions, have been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as diarrhea, fever, and sore throats.
